steps.title: Read the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: The line crosses the y-axis at the point (0, -1), so the y-intercept is $b = -1$. All four choices have $-1$ as the y-intercept, so we must determine the slope to distinguish them.

steps.title: Observe the slope direction
steps.content: The line descends from left to right, indicating a negative slope. This immediately eliminates choices A and B, which both have positive slopes $\frac{5}{8}$ and $\frac{8}{5}$.

steps.title: Calculate the slope using two points
steps.content:
Reading from the graph, the line passes through (0, -1) and (8, -6). Using the slope formula:
$$m = \frac{-6 - (-1)}{8 - 0} = \frac{-5}{8} = -\frac{5}{8}$$
The equation is $f(x) = -\frac{5}{8}x - 1$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
